fundamentals.name: MP Materials Corp.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MP Materials Corp., together with its subsidiaries, produces rare earth materials in the Western Hemisphere. It operates in two segments, Materials and Magnetics. The Materials segment owns and operates the Mountain Pass Rare Earth Mine and Processing facility located near Mountain Pass, San Bernardino County, California. The Magnetics segment produces magnetic precursor products, including NdPr metal; and manufactures NdFeB permanent magnets. MP Materials Corp. was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Las Vegas, Nevada.

judgment.summary: MP Materials shows explosive revenue growth and strong analyst sentiment, but suffers from negative operating margins and a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 118.6% YoY indicates significant top-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates are very high for the current quarter (152.9%) and fiscal year (229.6%).
Consensus analyst rating is 'strong buy' with a mean target of $80.40.
Strong liquidity with a current ratio of 7.18 and quick ratio of 6.51, and $1.74B cash on hand.
Last quarter's EPS beat estimates significantly (0.03 vs -0.04).
negatives: Operating margin is negative at -7.9%, and profit margin is -20.5%.
Forward P/E of 55.14 is very high, suggesting lofty growth expectations.
Free cash flow is negative at $-174.84M TTM.
Return on equity and assets are negative.

news.paragraph: MP Materials is facing headwinds despite a recent partnership with Apple and a new "overweight" rating from Barclays. Geopolitical tensions surrounding rare earth elements and insider sales have contributed to a stock decline, even as the company's valuation and growth estimates are being reassessed. The upcoming earnings report on August 6th will be critical for understanding the impact of these shifting expectations.
